description: Video recommendation algorithm based on readiness assessment results for Chapter 5: Sharing and Measuring (Division)

excellent_performance.rationale: Student has excellent understanding of multiplication and sharing, can fast-track to division concepts with challenge content

videoSequence.reason: Introduction to division as equal sharing
Learn basic division facts - dividend, divisor, quotient
Challenge: Long division with bigger numbers

ready_performance.rationale: Student is ready, provide complete coverage of division concepts

videoSequence.reason: Introduction to division as equal sharing

partial_readiness.rationale: Student has gaps in multiplication or sharing, needs targeted review before division concepts

adaptiveLogic.description: Select prerequisite videos based on specific weak concepts

exampleSequence.reason: Review: Multiplication basics and times tables
Review: Equal sharing and fair distribution
Start main topic: Division as equal sharing

not_ready.rationale: Student needs significant prerequisite review in multiplication and sharing before attempting division

videoSequence.reason: Foundation: Review multiplication basics and times tables
Critical: Understand repeated subtraction as division
Gentle intro to division as sharing
Begin main content: Basic division facts

postPlaylistAction.message: After watching these prerequisite videos, retake the readiness quiz to check if you are ready to continue with division.

conceptBasedRemediation.description: Map specific weak concepts to remedial videos

multiplication_basics.conceptName: Basic multiplication facts and times tables

equal_sharing.conceptName: Understanding equal sharing and fair distribution

comprehensionCheckpoints.description: Quick checks during video watching to adjust playlist

questions.question: In the problem 20 divided by 4 = 5, what is the quotient?
questions.explanation: The quotient is the answer to a division problem. Here, 5 is the quotient.

ifAllCorrect.message: Excellent! You understand basic division facts well.

ifAllWrong.reason: Student struggling with division facts, review multiplication

questions.question: If 7 x 8 = 56, what is 56 divided by 7?
questions.explanation: Since 7 x 8 = 56, we know 56 divided by 7 = 8. Multiplication and division are opposites!

ifCorrect.reason: Student ready for long division challenge

ifWrong.message: Watch the multiplication-division connection video again, then try some practice problems.

skip_ahead.action: Skip practice video, jump to challenge or next topic

add_support.action: Pause main topic, insert prerequisite review on multiplication basics

suggest_break.trigger: After 4 videos watched in a single session
suggest_break.action: Suggest taking a 5-minute break before continuing

recommend_practice.trigger: Student completes videos but scores < 70% on checkpoint
recommend_practice.action: Recommend practising division facts with real objects at home

recommend_retake.trigger: Student in not_ready category completes all prerequisite videos
recommend_retake.action: Strongly recommend retaking readiness assessment before main content

high_score.message: Excellent! You know your multiplication and sharing really well. You are ready to learn about division. We have created a fast-track playlist with challenge content for you.

good_score.message: Good work! You understand multiplication and sharing well. Let's learn about division with complete coverage of all the topics.

partial_score.message: You have some gaps in multiplication or sharing. Don't worry! We will start with targeted review videos on the concepts you need, then move to division.

low_score.message: You need to review multiplication basics and sharing first. We have created a playlist focused on building a strong foundation. After watching, you can retake the quiz and then learn division.

conceptProgressionMap.description: Shows how division concepts build on each other

playlistCompletion.description: % of students who watch >= 80% of recommended videos

practiceQuizPassRate.description: % of students who score >= 70% on practice quiz after watching playlist

videoRelevanceRating.description: Average student rating (out of 5) for playlist helpfulness

timeToMastery.description: Average minutes from readiness quiz to practice quiz pass

divisionFactsFluency.description: % of students who can solve basic division facts within 10 seconds

multiplicationDivisionLink.description: % of students who can use multiplication to solve a division problem

implementationNotes.recommendedValidation: Test with 15-20 Grade 4 students across all readiness levels

expectedOutcomes.excellent_students: Complete in 40-50 minutes with 90-100% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.ready_students: Complete in 35-40 minutes with 80-90% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.partial_students: Complete in 50-55 minutes with 70-80% practice quiz score (after prerequisite review)
expectedOutcomes.not_ready_students: Require prerequisite review (25 min) + retake + main content (25 min) = 50-60 total minutes, expected 60-75% on practice quiz

commonChallenges.solution1: Emphasise that division is sharing equally into groups, not taking away. Use visual grouping examples.
commonChallenges.challenge2: Students forgetting which number is the divisor and which is the dividend
commonChallenges.solution2: Use real-world language: 'I have 12 sweets (dividend) and I want to share among 3 friends (divisor).'
commonChallenges.challenge3: Students who know multiplication but cannot reverse it for division
commonChallenges.solution3: Practice fact families: 3 x 4 = 12, so 12 / 3 = 4 and 12 / 4 = 3.
